claim: Verification confirms that gathered evidence is sufficient to support PRD readiness. Both explorer and cross-checker provided consistent, well-sourced evidence confirming the acceptance criteria is met: agents can use WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence marked scope=external, while target-project facts still require project-scoped path evidence. No assumptions are presented as facts, all evidence paths are usable and verified, and missing data is properly reflected.

evidence.summary: DEMAND_EVIDENCE_RESULT_SCHEMA_DEFINITION explicitly requires scope field with four valid values: project, external, user, unknown. Field definition states project facts require project-scoped evidence.
evidence.why: Schema contract evidence proving scope distinction is built into the evidence result definition

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordScope classifies evidence as external when url field matches http/https OR source matches external_web/webfetch/websearch patterns.
evidence.why: Core classification logic showing how WebFetch/WebSearch evidence is detected as external

evidence.summary: evidenceRecordHasProjectLocator validates project-scope evidence requires concrete path; explicitly returns false if path matches http/https pattern.
evidence.why: Validation logic proving URLs cannot serve as project locators even if marked as scope=project

evidence.summary: projectEvidenceBlockingIssues generates PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker when evidence is only external/user/unknown; generates PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker when project-scope evidence lacks concrete path.
evidence.why: Core validation logic enforcing external-only evidence cannot satisfy project fact requirements

evidence.summary: Test 'external-only evidence cannot satisfy existing project facts' confirms evidence with scope=external triggers PROJECT_FACT_REQUIRES_PROJECT_EVIDENCE blocker.
evidence.why: Test evidence proving external-only evidence cannot satisfy project fact requirements

evidence.summary: Test 'external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence' confirms evidence with url field but marked as scope=project triggers PROJECT_EVIDENCE_PATH_REQUIRED blocker.
evidence.why: Test evidence proving external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence

evidence.summary: Agent prompt instructs: 'If the demand explicitly asks for external research/fetch/search, actually use WebFetch/WebSearch when available and record those records as scope=external' and 'WebFetch/WebSearch are allowed for external research; mark those records as scope=external and do not use them as project facts'.
evidence.why: Agent prompt evidence showing WebFetch/WebSearch are explicitly allowed but must be marked scope=external

evidence.summary: Doctrine states: 'project evidence must come from target项目的 code, tests, docs, config, logs or artifacts, and must carry repo-relative path or file locator; external evidence can only come from WebFetch/WebSearch or external docs, but can only be used as background research'.
evidence.why: Product doctrine evidence confirming external research cannot prove target-project facts

evidence.summary: Two test scenarios comprehensively cover external evidence validation: external-only evidence blocking for project facts, and external URL path validation rejection.
evidence.why: Test coverage evidence showing validation logic is exercised by tests

result.notes: Verification complete. Explorer and cross-checker provided consistent, well-sourced evidence with no conflicts. All 10 evidence records have explicit scope declarations, usable paths/lines, and clear justification. Schema defines four scopes with project facts requiring project-scoped evidence. Router classifies external evidence from URLs and webfetch/websearch patterns. Validation logic requires project evidence to have concrete file paths, not URLs. Tests confirm external-only evidence blocks project claims and external URLs cannot be relabeled as project evidence. Agent prompts explicitly instruct WebFetch/WebSearch usage for external research but mark as scope=external and not use as project facts. Doctrine confirms external research is background only. Acceptance criteria met: agents can use WebFetch/WebSearch as external evidence marked scope=external, and target-project facts still require project-scoped path evidence. No blockers or missing data identified. PRD readiness criteria satisfied.
